sections.paragraph:
to go around, to travel about, a country, followed by an acc. of the country, Gen 34:10, 21 Gen 34:21. (Kindred is סָהַר which see. Ch. סְחַר is very often in the Targums for the Heb. סָבַב. In Syriac it means spec. to travel about as a beggar, to go a begging. In Arab. ساحر and سخر the idea of going about is very uncertain, and it is not supported by the usage of the language). Specially to go round, to travel about countries for the sake of traffic; hence to trade, ἐμπορεύομαι. Gen 42:34, וְ֦אֶת־הָאָרֶץ תִּסְחָרוּ “and ye shall go through the land,” i.e. to buy corn. Part. סֹחֵר a chapman, merchant, ἔμπορος, Gen 23:16 37:28 . סֹחֲרֵי הַמֶּלֶךְ the king’s merchants, who made journeys in order to purchase for him, 1Ki 10:28 2Ch 1:16 also a sailor, Pro 31:14 Isa 23:2. Fem. סֹחֶרֶת a female merchant, Eze 27:12, 16 Eze 27:16, 18 Eze 27:18. Metaph. to have intercourse with any one, Isa 47:15. (In Aramean and Arabic the signification of trading is expressed by the cognate verb תְּגַר, تَجَرَ).

Pilpel סְחַרְחַר to go round quickly, used of the heart, i.e. to palpitate violently, Psa 38:11.

Derivatives מִסְחָר and the four nouns which follow.
